critic_findings.message: print() appends a trailing newline, so stdout is '{...}\n' not '{...}'. The reference shape (rq session_start_emit.py:283) uses sys.stdout.write() which emits no trailing newline. The spec says 'single JSON document on stdout, no trailing newline-separated extras' — print violates this.

import sys is placed inside the _run() helper function body rather than at module top-level. All imports must be at module scope per PEP 8; an in-function import is a code quality smell and will be flagged by ruff/isort.

findings_details.title: except ValueError does not guard against non-dict JSON root — payload.get() raises AttributeError
findings_details.body: json.load(stdin) can succeed and return a non-dict (list, integer, string, null) when stdin contains valid JSON that is not an object. The except ValueError: on line 126 only catches malformed JSON. If stdin is e.g. '[1,2,3]' or '42', payload is set to a list/int, and payload.get('cwd') on line 38 raises AttributeError uncaught by run(). Fix: after json.load succeeds, validate isinstance(payload, dict); fall back to {} if not. Also add a test covering this path (e.g. raw_stdin='[1,2,3]').

findings_details.title: _run silently ignores cwd when raw_stdin is also supplied
findings_details.body: _run(cfg, cwd=..., raw_stdin=...) silently drops the cwd argument because the raw_stdin branch constructs buf from raw_stdin with no reference to cwd. A future test author who writes _doc(cfg, cwd='/foo', raw_stdin='...') will get a test that does not exercise the cwd path they intended. Fix: add an assertion (or raise ValueError) at the top of _run if both raw_stdin and cwd are non-None, to make the conflict explicit.

findings_details.title: Repeated json.loads parse pattern should be extracted to _doc() helper
findings_details.body: The two-liner '_, output = _run(...); doc = json.loads(output)' appears 9 times across the test file (lines 34-35, 55-56, 64-65, 72-73, 84-85, 121-122, 133-134, 146-147 and implicitly line 44 for the disabled path). A _doc(cfg, cwd=None) helper that returns the parsed dict directly would eliminate the repetition. Fix: add 'def _doc(cfg, cwd=None): _, out = _run(cfg, cwd); return json.loads(out)' above the tests and replace each occurrence.

findings_details.title: test_disabled_emits_no_output duplicates test_disabled_prints_nothing
findings_details.body: test_disabled_emits_no_output (line 154) and test_disabled_prints_nothing (line 41) have identical setup (disabled Config, _run call) and identical assertion (output == ''). The new test adds no new scenario. Fix: remove test_disabled_emits_no_output (the new docstring 'no JSON, no whitespace' could be folded into the existing test's inline comment if the distinction matters).

findings_details.title: test_repo_block_when_pages_exist dropped page-path assertion without replacement
findings_details.body: The old test asserted both 'pages exist for myrepo' AND 'repos/myrepo/overview.md' in the output, verifying that the page listing actually enumerates individual pages. The new version at line 56 only checks the header line. Neither test_system_message_carries_repo_block nor any other test verifies that the per-page path appears in systemMessage or additionalContext. If _repo_block() stops emitting page lines, no test catches it. Fix: add 'assert repos/myrepo/overview.md in doc[systemMessage]' to test_repo_block_when_pages_exist at line 56.

findings_details.title: No comment explaining why repo_block appears in both systemMessage and additionalContext
findings_details.body: Lines 133-137 intentionally duplicate repo_block into both systemMessage (visible banner) and additionalContext (background session context). Without a comment, this reads as a bug — a future maintainer will deduplicate it, breaking the banner behavior. Fix: add a one-line comment above line 133: '# systemMessage surfaces the repo block as a visible banner; additionalContext carries instructions + repo block as background context'.

findings_details.title: bare except Exception silently swallows non-JSON errors on stdin
findings_details.body: Lines 124-127: the bare 'except Exception: payload = {}' catches json.JSONDecodeError (expected) but also UnicodeDecodeError, IOError, and any other error from json.load(). A UnicodeDecodeError on stdin is not the same as 'no payload' — it signals Claude Code provided unexpected binary input. The fallback to empty payload and os.getcwd() produces valid output, so no user-visible breakage occurs, but the diagnostic signal is lost. The exception should either be narrowed to (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) — which covers all JSON parse errors — or at minimum emit a warning to stderr before falling back. No test covers the case where json.load raises a non-JSON-decode error.

findings_details.title: test_repo_block_when_pages_exist drops assertion that page filenames appear in output
findings_details.body: The old test asserted both 'pages exist for myrepo' AND 'repos/myrepo/overview.md' in output, verifying that the page-listing loop in _repo_block produces entries for each .md file. The refactored test (line 54-56) drops the 'repos/myrepo/overview.md' assertion and only checks the header line. test_system_message_carries_repo_block (line 139-151) also does not assert that individual page filenames appear. The body of _repo_block — specifically _list_pages() and the per-page loop with _annotate() — is no longer verified to appear in any envelope field. Fix: add assertion that 'repos/myrepo/overview.md' appears in doc['systemMessage'] or doc['hookSpecificOutput']['additionalContext'] in either test_repo_block_when_pages_exist or test_system_message_carries_repo_block.

findings_details.title: no test covers the json.load exception path in run()
findings_details.body: The _run() helper always passes valid JSON to stdin (io.StringIO(json.dumps(payload))). No test exercises the except Exception branch at session_start.py:126 where json.load raises. This path falls back to payload={} and then cwd=os.getcwd(), ultimately emitting a valid JSON envelope with an empty systemMessage and instructions-only additionalContext. The behavior is safe but untested. Fix: add a test that passes an io.StringIO('not json') as stdin and asserts: (1) run() returns 0, (2) output is valid JSON, (3) systemMessage is empty string, (4) additionalContext contains the instructions block.

findings_details.title: plugin.json references a CHANGELOG.md that does not exist
findings_details.body: The description field ends with 'Changelog: https://github.com/arqu-co/agent-plugins/blob/main/plugins/risklab/CHANGELOG.md' but no CHANGELOG.md file exists under plugins/risklab/. Both peer plugins (producer, rq) have a CHANGELOG.md at that path. The URL is a dead link that will 404 for any user who follows it. Fix: create plugins/risklab/CHANGELOG.md with an initial entry for v0.1.0, matching the format producer uses.

findings_details.title: Bare issue reference risklab#1337 violates URL discipline rule
findings_details.body: Line 22 uses 'risklab#1337' as a parenthetical reference. This is not tappable on mobile and violates url-discipline.md which requires full https:// URLs in all user-facing output including skill markdown. The SKILL.md is rendered content, not a commit message body or PR description (the only exceptions). Fix: replace with the full URL https://github.com/arqu-co/risklab/issues/1337. The same issue applies at lines 98, 113, and 114 — all four bare refs must be expanded.

findings_details.title: plugin.json description references PR #454 as a bare ref
findings_details.body: The description field contains '(#454)' — a bare PR ref. The url-discipline rule permits bare #N refs only inside commit message bodies and PR descriptions where the git context makes them auto-linkable. A plugin.json description field is displayed in the marketplace UI and does not auto-link. Fix: expand to the full URL https://github.com/arqu-co/agent-plugins/pull/454, or drop the PR ref entirely (the version and date are sufficient changelog context in this field).

findings_details.title: .gitignore: .qa added without slash is scope drift and slightly redundant
findings_details.body: The PR adds '.qa' (no trailing slash) to .gitignore. The entry '.qa/' was already present at line 30, added in a prior commit specifically for the .qa symlink created by the rq-state-migrate hook. '.qa' (without slash) matches both a file and a directory, while '.qa/' matches only directories — so adding '.qa' is not strictly wrong, but it is a no-op for the existing directory case. More importantly, this change is unrelated to the risklab plugin and belongs in a standalone commit. If a plain file named .qa needs to be ignored, that should be explained; if not, this entry is unnecessary. Fix: remove the '.qa' line (without slash) and leave '.qa/' in place. If a non-directory .qa file genuinely exists and must be gitignored, move this change to a separate PR with a clear rationale.

findings_details.title: get_project called with guessed id when user provides only a project name
findings_details.body: Step 1 of 'Resolving the project' says: 'If they gave a name, call get_project with the id you believe matches to confirm.' But get_project takes a numeric id, not a name (confirmed by the MCP tools table: 'Fetch a single project's detail by id'). If the user provided only a name (no id), the agent has no id to pass — it would have to guess. The correct flow for a name-only input is: call list_projects, find the matching name, extract the id, then use it. Calling get_project with a guessed id is both logically broken and could silently use the wrong project. Fix: split into two cases. (A) User gives name AND id: use get_project(given_id) to verify. (B) User gives name only: call list_projects, find the match by name, use that id directly — get_project is unnecessary.

findings_details.title: CHANGELOG.md linked in plugin.json description does not exist
findings_details.body: plugin.json description field includes: 'Changelog: https://github.com/arqu-co/agent-plugins/blob/main/plugins/risklab/CHANGELOG.md'. This file does not exist in the repository. The URL will 404. Fix: either create plugins/risklab/CHANGELOG.md with an initial entry for v0.1.0, or remove the Changelog line from the description until the file exists.

findings_details.title: Bare cross-repo issue refs violate url-discipline rule
findings_details.body: Lines 22, 98, 113, and 114 use bare cross-repo refs: 'risklab#1337', 'arqu-co/risklab#1337', 'arqu-co/risklab#1335'. Per .claude/rules/url-discipline.md, bare #N refs are only acceptable inside commit message bodies and PR descriptions where the current owner/repo is clear from git context. SKILL.md is neither — it's a knowledge-surface markdown file rendered on GitHub and loaded into agent context. Cross-repo refs (different repo: arqu-co/risklab) never auto-link from this repo regardless of the exception. Fix: replace all four refs with full https:// URLs: https://github.com/arqu-co/risklab/issues/1337 and https://github.com/arqu-co/risklab/issues/1335.

findings_details.title: shareable_assets_list filters expired assets, making expired-asset renewal impossible via MCP
findings_details.body: Line 37 states shareable_assets_list 'filters out' revoked/expired rows. An admin who wants to renew an already-expired asset (extend its expires_at) cannot find its asset_id via this tool because the row is filtered. The only recovery is knowing the asset_id from a prior response. The skill should note this gap and tell the agent to keep track of asset_ids or to use the UI when an asset has already expired. Without this note, the agent may tell an admin to use shareable_assets_update on an expired asset, then fail to provide the id. Fix: add a note in the MCP tools table or the edit/revoke flow section: 'If an asset has already expired, it will not appear in shareable_assets_list; the admin must use the UI or know the asset_id from a prior session.'

findings_details.title: Password rotation does not immediately invalidate outstanding HMAC verify tokens
findings_details.body: Line 90 describes the HMAC verify token as using 5-minute time buckets. If an admin rotates a password via PATCH, any verify tokens issued against the old password remain valid for up to 5 minutes. This is a real (if bounded) security window the skill doesn't disclose. An admin who rotates a password believing access is immediately cut off is mistaken — a recipient who obtained a verify token in the last 5 minutes can still access the content. Fix: add a note to the Security model section: 'Password rotation takes effect on the next verify-password exchange. Outstanding verify tokens remain valid until their 5-minute bucket expires; effective lock-out takes up to 5 minutes.'
